description_long: This is a digital multimeter, a handheld electronic measuring instrument commonly used for electrical diagnostics and troubleshooting. The device features a large rectangular display at the top, likely an LCD, for reading measurements, and a rotary dial in the center for selecting various testing modes such as voltage, current, and resistance. Below the dial are two input jacks, color-coded red and black, into which the corresponding red and black test probes are plugged. The probes are attached by flexible wires and have pointed metal tips for making contact with electrical circuits. The casing is primarily blue with red accents, and the back features warning symbols and a compartment, likely for batteries. The overall design is robust and ergonomic, suitable for use in garages, workshops, or on-site urban electrical maintenance.
description: A handheld digital multimeter with a large display, rotary selector dial, and red and black test probes attached by wires. The casing is blue with red accents and features ergonomic design elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the display, rotary dial, control buttons, and both test probes connected to the input jacks.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slim profile and the protruding test probes, with minimal detail visible on the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid blue back cover, warning labels, and the storage compartment, with the probes hanging to the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the thickness of the device and the side profile of the rotary dial and probe connectors.
